title: Meringium klabatense
taxonomy: Meringium klabatense is a species of flowering plant in the family Colchicaceae. It is native to Indonesia. Meringium klabatense is classified as a perennial plant that belongs to the genus Meringium. Within the Colchicaceae family, it shares characteristics with other flowering plants such as Colchicum and Merendera. Further research is required to confirm its exact classification within the family.
uses: Meringium klabatense is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. There is limited research available on the specific uses of Meringium klabatense, but it is believed to have antifungal and antibacterial properties. Some studies suggest that the plant may be used to treat various health issues, including infections and skin conditions.
